skill.name: autonomous-agents
skill.description: Guidance for designing autonomous AI agents with reliable loops, constrained tool use, goal decomposition, reflection, guardrails, and production safety checks.
skill.summary: Design reliable autonomous agents with constrained loops, guardrails, and evaluation habits.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported backtick execution at SKILL.md line 68, but the backticks wrap related skill names in Markdown prose, not executable commands. The weak cryptographic algorithm alert at SKILL.md line 3 has no supporting cryptographic context, and no prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The four static external command findings all point to SKILL.md line 68. That line lists related skill names inside Markdown backticks and does not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any executable command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 68 contains only Markdown-formatted related skill identifiers. There is no shell syntax, Ruby code, command construction, or user-controlled execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert Has No Evidence
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to SKILL.md line 3. That line is a natural language description about autonomous agent reliability and contains no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or key handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced line is prose in front matter, not code. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms in the only scanned file.

content.value_statement: Autonomous agent projects often fail when steps, tools, and errors compound without control. This skill helps design constrained agent loops with guardrails, evaluation, and recovery patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains ReAct loops that alternate reasoning and tool actions.
Covers Plan-Execute designs that separate planning from execution.
Guides goal decomposition for bounded autonomous workflows.
Highlights reflection patterns for self-evaluation and iteration.
Identifies reliability guardrails, logging, cost limits, and validation needs.
Lists anti-patterns such as unbounded autonomy and trusting outputs blindly.
limitations: Provides conceptual guidance, not runnable agent framework code.
Does not include tests, scripts, or deployment templates.
The source text is brief and partially truncated in the front matter.
Requires users to adapt patterns to their tools, models, and risk tolerance.

use_cases.title: Review an Agent Architecture
use_cases.description: Assess a proposed autonomous agent loop for step count, failure modes, guardrails, and recovery behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan a Controlled Automation
use_cases.description: Turn a broad automation goal into a bounded Plan-Execute workflow with clear checkpoints and limits.

use_cases.title: Improve Agent Reliability
use_cases.description: Identify where logging, validation, ground truth checks, and least privilege controls should be added.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Agent Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain which autonomous agent pattern fits this goal: [describe goal]. Include the main loop, required guardrails, and likely failure modes.

prompt_templates.title: Decompose a Goal
prompt_templates.prompt: Break this autonomous agent goal into bounded steps: [goal]. Keep each step testable and list when human approval is required.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Reliability Risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent design for reliability risks: [design]. Focus on step count, compounding errors, tool failures, cost limits, and output validation.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production Guardrails
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production guardrail plan for this autonomous agent: [agent description]. Include least privilege, observability, rollback, evaluation, and escalation rules.

output_examples.input: Review an agent that researches vendors, emails candidates, and updates a CRM without approval.
output: Risk: The workflow combines research, external communication, and record updates in one autonomous loop.
Recommendation: Split the process into planning, draft review, approved send, and audited CRM update stages.
Guardrails: Add recipient allowlists, human approval before email, structured logging, and rollback for CRM changes.

output_examples.input: Design a support triage agent for incoming customer tickets.
output: Pattern: Use a Plan-Execute flow with classification, evidence gathering, draft response, and escalation decisions.
Limits: Restrict account actions, set confidence thresholds, and escalate billing or legal issues.
Evaluation: Compare classifications against a labeled ticket set before enabling automation.

output_examples.input: Improve an agent that fails after several tool calls.
output: Likely cause: Step-level errors compound across the workflow.
Fix: Reduce the loop length, validate each intermediate result, and stop when confidence drops.
Monitoring: Track tool errors, context usage, retries, cost, and final task success.

best_practices: Start with narrow agent tasks and expand autonomy only after measured reliability improves.
Use hard limits for cost, tool access, retries, context size, and execution time.
Validate outputs against ground truth and keep structured logs for every agent step.
anti_patterns: Giving an agent broad tool access before defining permissions and stop conditions.
Trusting autonomous outputs without validation, review, or evaluation data.
Combining planning, external actions, and permanent state changes in one unchecked loop.

faq.question: What does this skill help me design?
faq.answer: It helps design autonomous AI agent workflows that use bounded loops, guardrails, validation, and recovery steps.

faq.question: Does this skill provide runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture and review guidance, not a complete agent framework or deployment package.

faq.question: Which agent patterns are covered?
faq.answer: It covers ReAct loops, Plan-Execute flows, goal decomposition, reflection, and production reliability practices.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is model-agnostic and can support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What are the main safety recommendations?
faq.answer: Use least privilege, hard limits, structured logging, human checkpoints, and validation against reliable ground truth.

faq.question: Why does it warn about compounding errors?
faq.answer: Each extra autonomous step can reduce total success, so shorter loops and validation checkpoints improve reliability.
